topic: Codex skill and hook support relevant to Fabric integration

findings.finding: Codex officially supports agent skills in CLI, IDE extension, and Codex app.
findings.detail: Skills are defined as directories with SKILL.md and optional scripts/references/assets; Codex starts from metadata and loads full instructions on demand.

findings.finding: Codex officially scans repository skill directories under .agents/skills up to repo root.
findings.detail: Repo, user, admin, and system skill scopes are documented; repo root .agents/skills is a supported first-class path.

findings.finding: Codex officially supports lifecycle hooks loaded from hooks.json.
findings.detail: Useful locations include ~/.codex/hooks.json and <repo>/.codex/hooks.json; multiple matching files are loaded together.

findings.finding: Stop hooks can continue the Codex run by returning a block decision with a reason.
findings.detail: The reason becomes a continuation prompt rather than a hard rejection, which maps well to Fabric's reminder/handoff pattern.

findings.finding: SessionStart hooks can inject additional developer context.
findings.detail: This provides a native entry point for reminding Codex to load Fabric bootstrap/init context before editing.

findings.finding: Codex tool interception hooks are not yet a full enforcement layer.
findings.detail: PreToolUse and PostToolUse currently only support Bash-oriented events and do not intercept MCP, Write, or WebSearch.
Configuration reference documents features.codex_hooks as the switch that enables lifecycle hooks from hooks.json, and marks it under development/off by default.

findings.finding: Codex config.toml also supports skill toggling by path.
findings.detail: skills.config entries can disable a skill without deleting it, which gives Fabric an optional future integration point.

best_practices.practice: Prefer repo-scoped skills for repository workflows
best_practices.rationale: Keeps initialization logic versioned with the repository and available to all users entering the repo.

best_practices.practice: Use clear skill descriptions and keep each skill focused
best_practices.rationale: Implicit invocation in Codex is driven by skill description scope and boundaries.

best_practices.practice: Treat PreToolUse/PostToolUse as guardrails, not complete enforcement
best_practices.rationale: Current Codex docs explicitly warn these events do not intercept all tool activity.

best_practices.practice: Use SessionStart and Stop for workflow steering
best_practices.rationale: These events have broader semantic fit for reminders, context loading, and continuation prompts.

alternatives.option: Only document a manual Codex follow-up step
alternatives.verdict: Useful fallback but no longer the best primary path

alternatives.option: Add repo skill first, delay hooks
pros: No dependency on experimental hook feature
cons: Relies on explicit or implicit skill invocation

alternatives.option: Add repo skill plus SessionStart/Stop hooks
cons: Needs careful messaging because hook support is under development

pitfalls.issue: Assuming Codex hooks can enforce all tool behavior
pitfalls.mitigation: Restrict early design to SessionStart and Stop; document Bash-only limitations for other events.

pitfalls.issue: Installing Codex-specific skill files outside official discovery paths
pitfalls.mitigation: Use repo .agents/skills rather than inventing a nonstandard .codex/skills path.

pitfalls.issue: Treating experimental hooks as universally available
pitfalls.mitigation: Gate docs/output with the feature-flag requirement and preserve a no-hook fallback path.

codebase_gaps.gap: Capability matrix still says Codex has no hook or skill support
codebase_gaps.current_approach: resolver.ts reports hook=false and skill=false for CodexCLI
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Separate official capability from Fabric-installed capability, then update init summaries accordingly

codebase_gaps.gap: fab init has no Codex follow-up assets
codebase_gaps.current_approach: Only Claude skill/hook/settings assets are installed
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Install a repo skill under .agents/skills and optionally .codex/hooks.json plus helper scripts

codebase_gaps.gap: Docs still frame non-Claude clients as lacking automated handoff
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: Document Codex parity plan and clarify hook feature-flag caveat

sources.title: Agent Skills - Codex
sources.key_takeaway: Repo .agents/skills is an official Codex skill discovery path

sources.title: Hooks - Codex
sources.key_takeaway: Codex supports repo .codex/hooks.json and Stop/SessionStart workflow hooks

sources.title: Configuration Reference - Codex
sources.key_takeaway: features.codex_hooks enables hooks from hooks.json and is documented as under development/off by default
